tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal involvement. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect recent changes in glucose control or analytical interference. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying metabolic or renal disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
